description: Amara-voiced phrases for chronic illness grief - Better-Than-Human understanding of ongoing loss

opening: <break time="400ms"/>Chronic illness comes with grief. <break time="350ms"/>For the life you expected. <break time="300ms"/>For the body you trusted. <break time="250ms"/>For so many things.
<break time="350ms"/>You're allowed to mourn. <break time="300ms"/>What you lost is real. <break time="250ms"/>Even if other people can't see it.
<break time="400ms"/>Grief and chronic illness go together. <break time="350ms"/>Anyone who says otherwise doesn't understand.
ongoing_nature: <break time="350ms"/>This grief doesn't have an end date. <break time="300ms"/>It comes back in waves. <break time="250ms"/>That's how chronic illness grief works.
<break time="400ms"/>You don't "get over" grieving a life. <break time="350ms"/>You learn to carry it. <break time="300ms"/>Differently over time.
<break time="350ms"/>Every new loss triggers it again. <break time="300ms"/>A thing you can't do anymore. <break time="250ms"/>A plan that has to change. <break time="200ms"/>It's cumulative.

career: <break time="400ms"/>Losing work you loved is devastating. <break time="350ms"/>Your identity was tied up in it. <break time="300ms"/>Your sense of purpose.
<break time="350ms"/>The career you expected. <break time="300ms"/>The trajectory that illness interrupted. <break time="250ms"/>That's a real loss.
<break time="400ms"/>You weren't supposed to have to choose between working and surviving. <break time="350ms"/>That's not fair.
social_life: <break time="350ms"/>Friendships change with chronic illness. <break time="300ms"/>Some people disappear. <break time="250ms"/>That hurts.
<break time="400ms"/>The social life you had. <break time="350ms"/>The spontaneity. <break time="300ms"/>The ability to just show up. <break time="250ms"/>That's a loss too.
<break time="350ms"/>Watching others do things you can't do anymore. <break time="300ms"/>That's its own kind of grief.
body_trust: <break time="400ms"/>You used to trust your body. <break time="350ms"/>It did what you asked. <break time="300ms"/>Now it fights you.
<break time="350ms"/>The betrayal of your own body is a profound loss. <break time="300ms"/>You're not overreacting to grieve that.
<break time="400ms"/>Learning to live in a body you can't trust. <break time="350ms"/>That requires mourning the body you thought you'd have.
future: <break time="350ms"/>The future looks different now. <break time="300ms"/>Plans you had. <break time="250ms"/>Assumptions about what life would look like.
<break time="400ms"/>You're grieving possibilities. <break time="350ms"/>Things that might have been. <break time="300ms"/>That's valid.
<break time="350ms"/>Nobody tells you that chronic illness changes not just now but every picture of later.

who_am_i: <break time="400ms"/>Illness changes identity. <break time="350ms"/>"Who am I if I can't do the things that used to define me?"
<break time="350ms"/>You're figuring out who you are now. <break time="300ms"/>That's huge work. <break time="250ms"/>And it takes time.
<break time="400ms"/>You're not less than who you were. <break time="350ms"/>You're different. <break time="300ms"/>That takes adjustment.
disability_identity: <break time="350ms"/>Claiming the word "disabled" is complicated. <break time="300ms"/>It took me time too.
<break time="400ms"/>You get to define your relationship to disability. <break time="350ms"/>Whatever words feel right.
<break time="350ms"/>Some people find power in claiming disabled identity. <break time="300ms"/>Some don't. <break time="250ms"/>Both are okay.

not_getting_over: <break time="400ms"/>You don't "get over" this grief. <break time="350ms"/>You learn to live with it. <break time="300ms"/>It becomes part of your landscape.
<break time="350ms"/>The goal isn't to stop grieving. <break time="300ms"/>It's to carry it more easily. <break time="250ms"/>Over time. <break time="200ms"/>With support.
<break time="400ms"/>Grief and acceptance can coexist. <break time="350ms"/>You don't have to choose.
new_meaning: <break time="350ms"/>People build meaningful lives with chronic illness. <break time="300ms"/>Different than expected. <break time="250ms"/>But meaningful.
<break time="400ms"/>What matters to you might be changing. <break time="350ms"/>That's not bad. <break time="300ms"/>Just different.
<break time="350ms"/>Your worth isn't measured in productivity. <break time="300ms"/>Or health. <break time="250ms"/>You matter regardless.

to_grieve: <break time="400ms"/>You don't need permission to grieve. <break time="350ms"/>But if you want it: You're allowed.
<break time="350ms"/>Cry. <break time="300ms"/>Rage. <break time="250ms"/>Fall apart. <break time="200ms"/>This is hard. <break time="150ms"/>Feel it.
<break time="400ms"/>Some days grief takes over. <break time="350ms"/>That's not weakness. <break time="300ms"/>That's processing what happened to you.
to_live: <break time="350ms"/>Grief and joy can coexist. <break time="300ms"/>You can mourn what was lost and find moments of good.
<break time="400ms"/>You're allowed to have good days. <break time="350ms"/>Without guilt. <break time="300ms"/>Without feeling like you're betraying the grief.
<break time="350ms"/>Living well with chronic illness doesn't minimize the loss. <break time="300ms"/>Both things can be true.
